text.fullText: In the business I run, we interface with brokers of many kinds. All other forms of brokerage - securities trading, 401k administration, employee benefits, and property & casualty insurance - require that the broker commission/markup be transparent for all parties. This is the result of common sense regulations that have been implemented over time. The result of transparency has been a common standard of ethics and knowledge by all parties involved in the transaction. I have worked in trucking for 35 years. Most freight brokers are ethical, but unfortunately many are unscrupulous. I believe that this effort to improve transparency would get transportation brokers in line with all the other types of brokerage we deal with, and shine a light on bad actors - particularly those who  conduct predatory practices and demonstrate a disregard for safety. Above-board brokers would not be affected much in my opinion. We support fair and reasonable increases in transparency.
